twitter_thread: 40% of coding tasks can be automated with LLMs by 2025. Start adapting.
GitHub's Copilot can complete 30% of coding tasks. Learn to use it.
Tokenomics shows 25% of token usage is wasteful. Optimize with data.
Over-reliance on LLMs costs $10k/month in errors. Avoid this mistake.
Collaborate with LLMs on 20% of tasks to boost productivity.
Retrain in explainability to reduce LLM errors by 15%.
What's your experience with LLMs in software engineering? #softwareengineering #ai
pack.linkedin: By 2025, 40% of coding tasks can be automated with LLMs. Tools like GitHub's Copilot can complete 30% of tasks, while approaches like Tokenomics reveal 25% of token usage is wasteful. To stay relevant, software engineers must learn to work with LLMs, avoiding over-reliance that can cost $10k/month in errors. What skills do you think are most critical for engineers to develop in this new landscape?
